§ I
The Difference
Page 02

Why this wins.

Six things that put First Edition in a category no one else is in. The whole argument, on one page — every claim that follows in this document is proof of one of these.

01

Sunday delivery.

Thirty-six hours from the final vow to the finished film. The industry standard is eight to twelve weeks. The turnaround is the marketing.

02

A bespoke identity per wedding.

Every couple gets an AI creative director. A custom visual identity — color, type, pacing, music, voice — is generated for their film and applied uniformly across every deliverable. Couture for the wedding film, at scale.

03

Twelve products from one wedding.

Family hero cuts. Vendor hero cuts. Living Film. Memory Vault. Heritage Restoration. The studio ships a constellation of artifacts, not a single video. No competing studio is delivering even half of these.

04

Margins closer to software than services.

Fifty-four percent less labor per wedding than the industry standard. One editor at First Edition produces the annual output of five at a traditional studio. This is the unit-economic foundation of the licensing thesis.

05

A two-person studio. Not a payroll.

Two cinematographers and a stack of AI agents. No editor team. No ingest day. No coordinator. No assistant. The flagship studio fits in a small office. The overhead structure is the moat — and the basis of the licensing offer to other operators.

06

An eighteen-month window.

Two years ago, this business could not have been built. Camera-to-cloud at consumer scale, frontier multimodal AI, agent infrastructure — all matured in the last 18 months. In 24 months, every wedding studio in America will be trying to build this. First Edition gets to be the brand they are trying to catch.

§ II
The Pipeline
Page 03

Shot & shipped.

If you blinked, you might miss the fact we shot and shipped a wedding. Every operation in the studio — from inquiry to final delivery — runs through an automated pipeline built on Frame.io camera-to-cloud and a stack of purpose-built agents. Two cinematographers, one wedding day. The rest of the studio runs itself.

— The Foundation
Frame.io Camera-to-Cloud is the spine. Every camera uploads to the cloud during the shoot — no SD cards, no ingest day, no waiting. From the moment the shutter opens, every agent in the pipeline is already at work.
01
Pre-Wedding
AI · 6 agents
Inquiry, onboarding, brainstorming, style guide, contract, logistics.
02
Wedding Day
Humans · 18 hrs
Two cinematographers shoot. Camera-to-cloud uploads in real time.
03
Overnight Post
AI · 7 agents
Ingest, selects, assembly, color, audio, music, titles — all automated.
04
Sunday Review
Human · 4 hrs
Creative director final pass. The single irreducible human touch.
05
Delivery & Growth
AI · 6 agents
Multi-format delivery, social cuts, vendor press kits, SEO, marketing.
Phase 01
Pre-Wedding
AI · Fully Automated
  • Inquiry agent — qualifies leads, books discovery calls, answers FAQs in the studio's voice
  • Atelier onboarding agent — structured intake on couple, story, venue, family, aesthetic references
  • Brainstorming agent — async creative-director sessions with mood boards, music palettes, pacing studies
  • Style guide generator — locked aesthetic identity: color grade, type, pacing, music, voice, title design
  • Contract & booking automation — proposal, e-signature, payment, calendar — zero human-in-the-loop
  • Pre-day logistics agent — timelines, shot lists, vendor coordination, day-of brief generation
Phase 02
Wedding Day
Humans · Shoot Only
  • 1 lead cinematographer — primary coverage, key moments, hero shots
  • 1–3 second shooters — multi-angle ceremony & reception coverage per tier
  • Camera-to-cloud uplink — automatic, continuous, hands-off ingest during the day
  • On-set monitoring agent — alerts crew to gaps in coverage, missing audio, drained batteries
Phase 03
Overnight Post
AI · Fully Automated
  • Auto-organization — sorts, tags, syncs multi-cam footage by scene and timecode
  • First-pass selects — identifies key moments, hero shots, kissable b-roll, emotional beats
  • Style-aware assembly — builds highlight + documentary cuts using the couple's locked style guide
  • AI color grading — applies the couple's color identity uniformly across every clip
  • AI audio sweetening — dialogue isolation, leveling, ambient mixing, vow clarity
  • AI music selection — matches style-guide musical sensibility, fully-licensed library
  • Title & graphics — couple's names, date, venue, in the style-guide typography
Phase 04
Sunday Review
Human · 4 Hours
  • Creative director final pass — sequence nuance, emotional pacing, taste-level edits
  • Quality control — every deliverable reviewed before release, every cut signed off
Phase 05
Delivery & Growth
AI · Fully Automated
  • Auto-delivery — secure client portal, multi-format downloads, share-ready links
  • Social cut generator — 9:16, 1:1, 16:9 formats, captioned, platform-tuned
  • Vendor press kit — auto-tagged and auto-sent to planner, florist, venue, dress, band
  • SEO content generator — venue + season landing pages for the next bride searching
  • Marketing post automation — queued and scheduled across studio social channels
  • Memory book — auto-generated keepsake page with embedded film, branded to the style guide
— Growth · Capacity, not Headcount

Hire a freelancer. The system does the rest.

Because every operational decision runs through the agent stack, the studio scales with freelancers instead of employees. A second cinematographer for a Saturday in October. A specialist colorist for a destination wedding. The agents onboard them, brief them on the couple's style guide, route their schedule, and process their footage exactly the same as the in-house team.

The studio doesn't grow the way a service company grows — hiring full-time editors, coordinators, account managers. It grows the way a marketplace grows — adding capacity, not headcount. Booking, hiring, editing, deploying — all automated. A small core team and an elastic perimeter of contractors that expands and contracts with demand, at near-zero managerial cost.

Plate IV The pipeline, mid-Saturday-night
§ III
The Market
Page 04

A slow giant.

A sixty-six-billion-dollar industry whose customers are willing to spend, whose vendors are tiny, and whose delivery norms have not changed in twenty years.

The American wedding industry is enormous and stable. Two million weddings per year, an average couple-spend of $32,900, and a national total of $66 billion in 2025. Growth through 2030 is projected at a 6.8% CAGR.

Videography is an eight-percent line item of that budget. A median couple pays $4,000; the luxury tier pays $7,000 to $15,000+. The market for premium wedding video alone is roughly $4.2 billion annually.

The structural opportunity is fragmentation. Ninety percent of wedding vendors are small businesses. There is no Wedgewood of wedding film. The category has no scaled luxury operator and no technology platform — only thousands of one- and two-person studios delivering on the same eight-to-twelve-week timeline they have used since tape.

US weddings per year
2.0M
Average couple spend (2025)
$32.9K
Industry CAGR through 2030
6.8%
Median videography spend
$4.0K
Luxury videography ceiling
$15K+
Industry-standard delivery
8–12 wks
Plate I The Prospectus, as object
§ IV
The Atelier
Page 05

A bespoke wedding, at scale.

Every couple is paired with a proprietary AI creative director. Through structured discovery, the agent builds a complete visual identity for their wedding — and encodes it into every deliverable they receive.

The luxury moat is not just speed. It is that every couple receives a wedding film cut from cloth tailored to them — a coherent visual identity applied across the highlight, the documentary, the social pieces, and the press kit. Couture for the wedding film. At scale.

— Plate V · The Atelier Agent The AI creative director in session — color palette, mood, music, voice. The couple's aesthetic distilled into a working style identity the pipeline can apply.
01
Onboarding

The discovery

The agent conducts a structured intake — proposal story, venue, music, family, aesthetic references, fashion, anything that signals the couple's identity. What a planner gathers across a dozen meetings, the agent assembles in a single sitting.

02
Brainstorming

The direction

The agent presents options — visual references, mood boards, music palettes, pacing studies. The couple steers. Three asynchronous sessions, all archived. A creative-director relationship without the creative-director schedule.

03
Customization

The style guide

A locked aesthetic identity is generated — color grade, type treatment, pacing signature, music style, editorial voice, title design. A formal document the couple keeps. The blueprint every deliverable will be made from.

04
Application

The deliverables

The style guide is encoded directly into the production pipeline. Highlight, documentary, social cuts, press kit — every piece of media the couple receives shares one coherent identity. Nothing feels off-brand from itself.

— The Implication
The industry sells couples a wedding film. We sell them a wedding identity — applied with software discipline across every artifact of their day.
§ V
The Inventory
Page 06

Twelve deliverables.

The lineup of AI-powered products that ships from a single wedding day. Every couple receives more films, more identity, and more reach than any traditional studio can deliver — and the catalog ladders cleanly across the three tiers.

01

The Style Preview

Before contract: AI generates a 30-second mock film from the couple's Pinterest references, styled to their aesthetic. Couples see the look before they sign. The deal-closer no competitor can show.

Sales Tool
02

On-Set Coverage Agent

AI watches the live camera-to-cloud feed during the wedding and alerts crew to coverage gaps in real time. No moment goes unrecorded. Impossible without the cloud pipeline beneath it.

Standard
03

The Style Bible

The atelier-generated visual identity delivered as a printed-quality keepsake — color palette, type, music notes, mood references. The blueprint of their wedding, in their hands.

Standard
04

Family Hero Cuts

Five personalized 90-second films from the same footage — one for each parent set, one for the wedding party, one for the kids, one for the couple. Six perceived products from one wedding sale.

Standard
05

Vendor Hero Cuts

A 60-second cut auto-built for each vendor — florist, planner, venue, dress, band. Vendors push us to their next clients because no one else hands them content this good.

Standard
06

Phone Camera Augmentation

Guests' iPhone footage uploads to the pipeline, color-matched to the style guide, and incorporated into the final film. Two hundred cameras at the venue, at no marginal cost.

Heirloom+
07

The Vow Underscore

An original instrumental score generated from the vow audio itself, keyed to emotional beats. No licensing library — this music exists only for this couple, in the world, forever.

Heirloom+
08

Anniversary Drops

A new 60-second cut delivered automatically every anniversary, forever. Different music, different moments emphasized. A lifetime customer value engine at near-zero marginal cost.

Heirloom+
09

Heritage Restoration

Parents' and grandparents' wedding photos restored, colorized, and intercut at generational moments. The kiss — then a 1962 kiss of their grandparents, in the same color palette.

Legacy
10

The Living Film

The wedding film as a custom web app — not a video file. Re-cut from a phone by length, music, focus, or person. A queryable wedding no other studio in America can deliver.

Legacy
11

The Memory Vault

Every second of footage transcribed, tagged, and searchable. "Show me every shot of my dad laughing." "Find when we cut the cake." Permanent, private, queryable forever.

Legacy
12

Absent Family Cut

For the grandparent in hospice, the brother deployed overseas: a private link to a film that improves in near-real-time as the day unfolds. The story that wins the press write-up.

Legacy
Plate VI The Living Film · re-cut by length, music, or person
— The Implication
No competing studio is delivering even half of these. The wedding industry sells couples a film. We sell them a constellation of artifacts that surrounds the wedding for the rest of their lives — and a stream of marketing content that surrounds the studio for the rest of ours.
§ VI
The Workload
Page 07

The math of a wedding.

Per-wedding labor — industry standard versus First Edition pipeline. The hours below are the entire competitive moat in a single table.

A traditional studio spends roughly fifty person-hours producing a wedding film. We spend twenty-five. Two-thirds of the savings live in post-production — exactly where the industry's bottleneck has always been.

Workstream Traditional Studio First Edition Δ
Shoot day — lead operator10-hour wedding coverage 10.0 hr 10.0 hr —
Shoot day — second shooterceremony + reception coverage 8.0 hr 8.0 hr —
Ingest, backup, organizationautomated via camera-to-cloud 5.0 hr 0.0 hr −5.0 hr
First-pass selects + assemblyAI pipeline runs overnight 12.0 hr 0.5 hr −11.5 hr
Color gradingAI auto-grade + human polish 4.0 hr 1.0 hr −3.0 hr
Audio sweetening + mixAI dialogue isolation + leveling 3.0 hr 0.5 hr −2.5 hr
Editorial final passhuman creative direction 5.0 hr 3.0 hr −2.0 hr
Revisions cycleclient review & refinement 4.0 hr 1.0 hr −3.0 hr
QC + deliveryautomated client portal 2.0 hr 0.5 hr −1.5 hr
Total person-hours · per wedding 53.0 hr 24.5 hr −54%
— The Implication
One editor at First Edition produces the annual output of five editors at a traditional studio. The unit economics that result are closer to software than to services — and they are the entire foundation of the licensing thesis.
§ VII
The Pricing
Page 08

Three tiers.

Every package delivers Sunday. The tiers separate by scope of coverage, length of finished film, and depth of marketing deliverables. All pricing positions First Edition at the top of its regional market.

Tier I

The Signature

$6,500— starting
  • 8 hours coverage
  • 1 lead cinematographer + 1 second shooter
  • 4K, multi-cam, broadcast audio
  • 4–5 minute cinematic highlight film
  • All five Family Hero Cuts
  • Vendor Hero Cuts (auto-distributed)
  • The Style Bible keepsake
  • Delivered Sunday by noon
For the couple who wants the film, the speed, and nothing extra.
Tier II

The Heirloom

$9,500— starting
  • 10 hours coverage — prep through reception
  • 1 lead + 2 second shooters
  • 6–7 minute cinematic highlight film
  • 30–40 minute documentary edit
  • All Standard inventory, plus:
  • Phone Camera Augmentation
  • The Vow Underscore (original score)
  • Anniversary Drops (forever)
  • Drone aerial coverage
  • Same-night teaser, released that evening
  • Delivered Sunday by 10 AM
The full record. The film their grandchildren will watch.
Tier III

The Legacy

$14,500— starting
  • 12+ hours — rehearsal dinner through send-off
  • 1 lead + 3 second shooters
  • 7–8 minute hero cinematic film
  • 60–90 minute full feature edit
  • All Heirloom inventory, plus:
  • Heritage Restoration
  • The Living Film (custom web app)
  • The Memory Vault (searchable archive)
  • Absent Family Cut (real-time stream)
  • Live ceremony stream for absent guests
  • Drone + cinema-grade gimbal work
  • Delivered Sunday at sunrise
The full editorial treatment — the most-shared wedding film in the couple's circle.

Add-ons available across all tiers — raw footage license, second-day brunch coverage, custom score commissioning, additional revision rounds.

~$241K

Launch timeline

— Six Months to First Wedding Delivered

Month 1
Form & fund
Entity, contracts, insurance, capital commit, equipment ordering.
Month 2
Build the stack
AI pipeline deployment, camera-to-cloud testing, edit suite setup.
Month 3
Brand & portfolio
Identity, website, two styled portfolio shoots, social presence.
Month 4
Soft launch
Planner outreach, vendor relationships, first booked weddings.
Month 5–6
First deliveries
Live operation. Pipeline tuning. Playbook documentation begins.
§ IX
The Trajectory
Page 10

A three-year model.

A directional revenue model based on average package size of $9,500 and conservative booking ramp. Year Three onward, licensing revenue compounds on top of flagship operations.

Period Weddings Avg Package Studio Revenue License Revenue Total
Year OneFlagship launch · half-season 12–18 $9.5K $114–171K — $114–171K
Year TwoFull season · price discipline 40–55 $10.0K $400–550K — $400–550K
Year ThreeFlagship ceiling + Licensee 01 70–90 $10.5K $735–945K $60–120K $795K–1.0M
Year Four+Network scale-out flagship cap $11.0K+ $1.0M+ $300K+ $1.3M+

Estimates are directional and assume successful flagship execution. The Year Three license rollout is the inflection point where the business shifts from operator-bound to platform-leveraged.

§ X
The Partnership
Page 11

A company in two halves.

A clean operational division. Each partner owns the half they are uniquely built to run. Equal stake, equal say, equal authorship.

— Left Half

The Studio

[YOUR NAME] · Production & Technology

  • Production engine — cameras, crew, on-day execution
  • Proprietary AI editing pipeline & infrastructure
  • Camera-to-cloud workflow design & technology stack
  • Editorial quality control & creative direction
  • Technology roadmap toward the licensing platform
— Right Half

The Business

[PARTNER NAME] · Operations & Growth

  • Operations & client lifecycle — booking through delivery
  • Sales, business development & account management
  • Capital partnership & financial stewardship
  • Industry relationships, vendor & venue network
  • Growth strategy toward the licensing rollout
50 / 50. Equal stake. Equal say. Equal authorship.
Plate VII Sunday morning · the film, delivered
§ XI · Decision

The market is wide open.
The technology is ready.
The window is now.

First Edition is positioned to become the first scaled luxury operator in a sixty-six-billion-dollar industry that has, somehow, never had one. The capital required is modest. The runway to break-even is short. The platform thesis behind it is large.

The next step is a working session — review the full model, walk through the pipeline, agree the terms.
